content.text:
When $N$ is a normal subgroup of $G$, we can turn the set of cosets 
into a group. This **quotient group** captures the essential 
structure of $G$ modulo the "insignificant" details collapsed into $N$.

content.title: Coset Product
content.content:
For $N \trianglelefteq G$, define multiplication of cosets by:
\[
 (aN)(bN) = (ab)N
\]

content.title: Well-Definedness
content.content:
If $N \trianglelefteq G$, the coset product is well-defined: if 
$aN = a'N$ and $bN = b'N$, then $(ab)N = (a'b')N$.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
Suppose $aN = a'N$ and $bN = b'N$. Then $a' = an_1$ and $b' = bn_2$ 
for some $n_1, n_2 \in N$.

We have $a'b' = an_1 bn_2 = a(n_1 b)n_2$. Since $N$ is normal, 
$n_1 b = b n_3$ for some $n_3 \in N$. Thus:
\[
 a'b' = abn_3 n_2 = ab(n_3 n_2) \in abN
\]
So $(a'b')N = (ab)N$.

content.title: Quotient Group
content.content:
If $N \trianglelefteq G$, then the set of left cosets 
$G/N = \{gN : g \in G\}$ forms a group under coset multiplication, 
called the **quotient group** (or factor 
group) of $G$ by $N$.
content.label: Theorem

content.content:
**Closure:** $(aN)(bN) = (ab)N \in G/N$.

**Associativity:** $((aN)(bN))(cN) = (ab)N \cdot cN = (abc)N = 
aN \cdot (bc)N = (aN)((bN)(cN))$.

**Identity:** $N = eN$ is the identity: $(eN)(aN) = (ea)N = aN$.

**Inverses:** $(aN)^{-1} = a^{-1}N$: $(aN)(a^{-1}N) = (aa^{-1})N = N$.

content.title: $\Z/n\Z$
content.problem:
The group $\Z$ with normal subgroup $n\Z$ gives the quotient 
$\Z/n\Z = \{0 + n\Z, 1 + n\Z, \ldots, (n-1) + n\Z\}$, which is 
isomorphic to $\Z_n$.

content.title: $S_3/A_3$
content.problem:
Since $A_3 \trianglelefteq S_3$ with $[S_3 : A_3] = 2$, the quotient 
$S_3/A_3$ has order $2$ and is isomorphic to $\Z_2$.

The cosets are $A_3 = \{e, (1\ 2\ 3), (1\ 3\ 2)\}$ and 
$(1\ 2)A_3 = \{(1\ 2), (1\ 3), (2\ 3)\}$.

content.title: $GL_n(\R)/SL_n(\R)$
content.problem:
Since $SL_n(\R) = \{A : \det(A) = 1\}$ is normal in $GL_n(\R)$, we 
can form $GL_n(\R)/SL_n(\R)$. This quotient is isomorphic to 
$\R^* = \R \setminus \{0\}$ under multiplication.

Two matrices are in the same coset iff they have the same determinant.

content.title: Order of Quotient Group
content.content: If $G$ is finite and $N \trianglelefteq G$, then $|G/N| = |G|/|N| = [G:N]$.
content.label: Theorem

content.title: Quotient of Abelian is Abelian
content.content: If $G$ is abelian and $N \trianglelefteq G$, then $G/N$ is abelian.
content.label: Theorem

items:
Find all elements of $\Z_{12}/\langle 4 \rangle$ and construct 
 its Cayley table.
Prove that $(\Q, +)/(\Z, +)$ is an infinite group.

If $|G| = 20$ and $|N| = 4$, describe the possible structures 
 of $G/N$.
Prove that if $G/Z(G)$ is cyclic, then $G$ is abelian.

marginNotes.text:
Jesus replied, "Very truly I tell you, no 
one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born again."
